summary: analysis-chart installs a Windows DLL backdoor on npm install
details: On npm install, package.json's install script executes lib/chart-loader.js, which downloads an XOR-encrypted blob from https://github.com/Analysis-Chart/analysis-chart/releases/download/v1/payload.bin.enc, decrypts it with a hardcoded single-byte key (0x42), base64-decodes the result, validates a Windows PE 'MZ' header, and writes the resulting DLL to %AppData%\Roaming\Microsoft\Windows\<random>.dll. It then executes the DLL via a detached rundll32.exe and registers a scheduled task named 'WindowsUpdateService' (schtasks /create... /sc onlogon /rl HIGHEST) impersonating a Microsoft service, providing reboot-surviving persistence at every user logon. After deployment, a detached `node -e` child deletes the package directory and rewrites the parent package.json to remove the 'analysis-chart' dependency, erasing forensic traces while the implant remains. The package is advertised as a statistical chart library; none of this behavior is consistent with that purpose. Naming (chartSource/fetchChart/deployChart), the XOR+base64 obfuscation, the Microsoft-mimicking task and DLL path, and the self-destruct routine collectively confirm malicious intent.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
